name: Coleman false-vacuum decay
summary: Quantum-mechanical bubble nucleation through tunneling in field theory: a metastable 'false vacuum' decays via O(4)-symmetric instanton ('bounce') solutions. Coleman's semiclassical formula computes the decay rate per unit volume Gamma/V ~ A exp(-S_E/hbar) from the Euclidean action of the bounce. Foundational for understanding cosmological vacuum-stability tunneling; the gravitational generalisation (Coleman-de Luccia 1980) handles spacetime curvature.

addresses_puzzles.note: Provides the calculational framework for whether the SM electroweak vacuum is metastable on cosmological timescales. With current m_H, m_t the predicted lifetime tau ~ 10^600 yr is vastly longer than the universe age, so the SM vacuum is metastable but not dangerous. The framework is the standard one; the metastability conclusion follows from it.

introduces.note: A computational technique within QFT, not a new physical mechanism. Listed for graph completeness because it is what defines the vacuum-metastability puzzle's quantitative content.

history: Coleman (1977), 'Fate of the false vacuum: semiclassical theory', Phys. Rev. D 15, 2929 (with erratum PRD 16, 1248). Callan and Coleman (1977), 'Fate of the false vacuum II: first quantum corrections', PRD 16, 1762. Coleman and de Luccia (1980), 'Gravitational effects on and of vacuum decay', PRD 21, 3305 — included gravity. Standard tool ever since for inflationary cosmology, EW vacuum stability, and string-landscape tunneling.

references.citation: Coleman, 'Fate of the false vacuum: semiclassical theory', Phys. Rev. D 15, 2929 (1977)
Coleman and de Luccia, 'Gravitational effects on and of vacuum decay', Phys. Rev. D 21, 3305 (1980)
